data.title: Nvidia unveils £2B investment in UK AI startup scene
data.description:
On top of its $5B, mega investment in Intel (INTC), Nvidia announced on Thursday that it would be investing £2B ($2.7B) into the U.K.'s artificial intelligence startup scene.

Joining Nvidia in the investment will be a number of asset managers and venture capital firms, including Accel, Air Street Capital, Balderton, Hoxton Ventures and Phoenix Court, the tech giant said in a statement. The investments will be used to bring “new capital and advanced AI infrastructure” to major U.K. hubs like London, Oxford, Cambridge and Manchester. 

“This is the age of AI — the big bang of a new industrial revolution,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang explained. “The United Kingdom is in a Goldilocks moment, where world-class universities, bold startups, leading researchers and cutting-edge supercomputing converge. There has never been a better time to invest in the U.K. — AI is unlocking new science and sparking entirely new industries. With new capital and advanced infrastructure, we are doubling down to empower the U.K. to lead the next wave of AI innovation.”

“NVIDIA’s investment is a major vote of confidence in the U.K. both today and long into the future,” said U.K. Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer. “By backing our startups, empowering our researchers and connecting capital with talent, this partnership will create jobs, spark new industries and ensure the U.K. remains at the forefront of global AI leadership.”

Earlier this week, Nvidia said it and its partners — including U.K. AI startup Nscale — will be investing £11B in U.K. AI factories, with up to 120,000 of Nvidia's Blackwell GPUs. Nscale, ChatGPT maker OpenAI and Nvidia are also establishing Stargate U.K., which will feature Blackwell Ultra GPUs operating in Nscale’s U.K. data centers by 2026.

data.title: U.S. stocks rally due to Intel-Nvidia deal, a day after Fed's rate cut
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) and the S&P 500 (SP500) hitting new intraday highs, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.6% in afternoon trade, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.1%. Both notched all-time intraday highs. The Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

Markets were helped by strong premarket action from tech firms Intel (INTC) +23.2% and Nvidia (NVDA) +3.8% after the firms announced a blockbuster deal on Thursday to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Also, the FOMC on Wednesday announced its decision, trimming its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25% on Wednesday. Miran dissented, arguing for a 50-basis-point cut.

“The Federal Reserve is cutting interest rates during a time when stocks are at record highs and the economy is still growing, which is a very unique backdrop, as Fed cuts are typically associated with a problematic economy. This dynamic is bullish for stocks,” said Robert Schein, CIO at Blanke Schein Wealth Management.

“Now that rates have been moving lower and the Federal Reserve is looking to cut rates a few more times, we are even more bullish on big tech and financials stocks. Big tech stocks tend to outperform during lower interest rate environments, and financials may see a boost from additional M&A and mortgage activity that may come about from lower rates,” Schein added.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.11%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.58%.

Among Thursday's market movers, cybersecurity stocks were higher. CrowdStrike (CRWD) surged +12.8% after the company updated targets during its Fal.Con 2025 event.

On the economic side, initial jobless claims dropped more than expected in the past week. Also, the Philly Fed Manufacturing Index jumped in September, blowing past consensus.

The August U.S. Leading Economic Index fell more than expected in August, -0.5% MoM to 98.4.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Obamacare tax credits could expand budget deficit by $350B if extended, CBO says
data.description:
A permanent extension to expiring tax credits to recipients of Obamacare health plans could boost the number of people with health insurance but would add $350B to the U.S. budget deficit over ten years, the Congressional Budget Office said Thursday.

Its findings come as the enhanced subsidies that reduced health premiums of millions of enrollees who purchased health plans through the Affordable Care Act (ACA) marketplace are set to lapse at the end of 2025 if Congress fails to act.

The tax credits introduced in 2021 as part of the American Rescue Plan Act pandemic-relief law and then extended through the Inflation Reduction Act in 2022 could expand the U.S. budget deficits by $349.8B from 2026 to 2035, if they are permanently extended, CBO added.

However, it could also boost the number of insured Americans by 3.6M and 3.8B in 2030 and 2035, respectively, CBO said, citing its health insurance simulation model. Additionally, the agency estimated that if the permanent expansion takes effect on Sept. 30, premiums for the 2026 plan year could decline by 2.4% from its baseline projections.

The CBO projected in June that more than 4M Americans will become uninsured in 2034 if enhanced subsidies are allowed to lapse this year.

Operators in the ACA Marketplace include managed care organizations UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), CVS Health (CVS), Centene (NYSE:CNC), Cigna (CI), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) and Oscar Health (NYSE:OSCR)

data.title: Cross Country Healthcare gains on speculation it wasn't subject of FTC meeting
data.description:
Cross Country Healthcare (NASDAQ:CCRN) rose 5.5% amid speculation it wasn't the subject of a Federal Trade Commission closed-door meeting on Wednesday.

There was speculation prior to the meeting that Aya Healthcare's planned $615 million purchase of Cross Country (NASDAQ:CCRN) may be the subject of an  FTC closed door meeting on Wednesday.

Traders on Thursday told Seeking Alpha that the real subject of the meeting was likely a FTC lawsuit against Live Nation (LYV) and Ticketmaster after the suit was disclosed on Thursday. 

In March, Cross Country (NASDAQ:CCRN) received a second request from the FTC about the travel nursing company's planned sale to Aya. Cross Country (NASDAQ:CCRN) and Aya disclosed the companies had certified that they had substantially complied with the FTC's second request as of Aug. 29, according to an 8-K filing. The deal is expected to close in Q4

Aya Healthcare in December entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Cross Country for $18.61/share in cash.

data.title: PG&E raised to Equal Weight, Edison maintained Underweight at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
PG&E (NYSE:PCG) -1.1% in Thursday's trading despite winning an upgrade to Equal Weight from Underweight with a $20 price target, up from $19, at Morgan Stanley, which said it is now more comfortable with the utility's investment case, citing the replenishment of California's wildfire fund that appears to indicate more than one catastrophic fire would be needed to lead to a depletion of wildfire funds and a loss of key protections.

Morgan Stanley analyst David Arcaro said the replenished fund combined with the current ~50% discount to the sector's P/E offers a much more favorable risk/reward compared to his February downgrade, when the company was set to enter fire season with limited protection from the fund, which looked too risky for investors to bear.

While acknowledging PG&E's (NYSE:PCG) meaningful discount to industry peers and improved risk reward, Arcaro noted California still lacks a permanent solution for its significantly above average wildfire risk, adding that utility investors tend to apply meaningful discounts for tail risk scenarios which are not consistent with the financial stability expected for a utility investment.

Arcaro said he remains Underweight on Edison International (NYSE:EIX), citing lingering tail risks from the Eaton Fire, and the utility lacks some of the positive features seen in PG&E, such as "elevated flexibility of the financial plan and room to significantly reduce costs, creating bill headroom to increase capex without requiring relevant rate hikes for customers."

The analyst reiterated his Outperform rating on Sempra (SRE), seeing its exposure to fire risk as very low, even while the stock also has underperformed on the back of this year's Los Angeles-area fires, noting the company's better topography, early investment in wildfire mitigation, and much higher exposure to the gas side relative to its electric utility, with much excitement about the Texas business, which is among the best utility growth opportunities in the industry.

data.title: Wells Fargo CIO raises S&P 500 target, advises against small caps
data.description:
Darrell Cronk, chief investment officer for wealth and investment management at Wells Fargo, raised his year-end S&P 500 (SP500) target to between 6,600 and 6,800 amid new market record highs and the Federal Reserve’s easing cycle.

Cronk shared his market outlook during an interview with CNBC, noting that while he expects volatility to increase, the current economic conditions are favorable for continued growth.

The Federal Reserve’s recent interest rate cuts have created a positive market environment, according to Cronk. “High yield spreads are at fresh lows this morning,” he said, adding that banks (KBE), (KRE) are at their all-time highs despite the rate cuts – a rare occurrence that historically suggests economic strength rather than weakness.

He also was particularly optimistic about long-term market prospects. “2026 probably sets up to be an even better year,” he said, citing stable fiscal policies, continued monetary easing, and strong corporate balance sheets.

He emphasized that “the markets are telling you, ‘Hey, things are a go for the remainder of this year and into next year.’”

When discussing specific market segments, Cronk expressed skepticism about small caps (IWM) despite their recent outperformance. He acknowledged that small caps typically perform well in two scenarios: emerging from recessions and during aggressive Fed cutting cycles. However, he questioned the sustainability of their current strength, pointing to significant market cap disparities.

Cronk specifically recommended against rotating investments from technology (XLK) to small caps (IWM). “This whole idea that you’re going to rotate from tech to small cap…is, to us, a little bit nonsensical.”

He cited concerns about quality deterioration in the small cap universe, noting that “private capital’s come in there and cherry-picked a lot of the good companies and taken them back private.”

As a result of these concerns, Cronk confirmed that Wells Fargo recently moved to an underweight position on small caps (IWM), (SP600). “And we think that’s still the right trade to hold.”

data.title: Wynn Resorts is tipped by Stifel to stay in rally mode
data.description:
Stifel raised estimates on Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) on its view that the casino company has multiple catalysts in play.

Analyst Steven Wieczynski pointed to recent healthier trends emerging out of the Macau gaming market, including easier access into the market through improved visa schemes and further government stimulus. Macau GGR is seen rising for both the mass and VIP segments.

Despite the recent rally in Wynn (NASDAQ:WYNN) shares, Wieczynski said valuation remains underwhelming and continues to discount the recovery in Macau. "We think there is considerable upside left in WYNN and continue to believe this name should remain a core holding for any investor," wrote Wieczynski. Stifel boosted its price target moves on Wynn (WYNN) to $145 off higher 2026-2027 EBITDA estimates.

Shares of Wynn Resorts (WYNN) were up 2.8% in Thursday afternoon trading and are 17% higher over the last six weeks. Wall Street analysts have a consensus Strong Buy rating on WYNN, while Seeking Alpha analysts are down a notch at Buy.

data.title: Onex to buy Integrated Specialty Coverage from KKR
data.description:
Onex Corporation (TSX:ONEX:CA) said on Thursday that its Onex Partners Opportunity Fund agreed to acquire a tech-enabled insurance platform, Integrated Specialty Coverages ("ISC"), from KKR (NYSE:KKR). 

The investment by Onex Partners is being made alongside the Public Sector Pension Investment Board (“PSP Investments”), Ardian (through its Co-Investment strategy), and several other institutional equity partners. ISC’s management team and employees will remain significant shareholders following the transaction.

The insurance sector has been a core focus for Onex Partners.  "This investment continues Onex Partners’ long history of successfully investing across the entire property & casualty insurance value chain, particularly in founder-led businesses," the company said.

Onex (TSX:ONEX:CA) stock rose 0.8% in afternoon trading in Toronto.

Financial terms of the deal weren't disclosed. The transaction is expected to close later this year.

data.title: Coinbase launches USDC onchain lending product yielding as much as 10.8%
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) is introducing an onchain lending product that lets users earn yields of up to 10.8% on their USDC (USDC-USD) stablecoin deposits, it said on Thursday.

The new feature will be rolled out to users over the coming weeks in the U.S. (excluding NY State), Bermuda, and additional countries, the crypto exchange said in a blog post.

When a user deposit its USDC (USDC-USD), Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) will create a smart contract wallet to connect to the Morpho protocol through onchain vaults managed by Steakhouse Financial, where the funds will be allocated across various lending markets. Funds are always accessible and can be withdrawn at any time when liquidity permits.

Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) already offers users passive USDC (USDC-USD) rewards of 4.1% annual percentage rate, or 4.5% for Coinbase One members, it said.

USDC (USDC-USD) is the dollar-backed stablecoin issued by Circle Internet Group (CRCL).

data.title: Immunocore initiated at neutral at Guggenheim as Kimmtrak priced into shares
data.description:
• Guggenheim has initiated Immunocore Holdings (NASDAQ:IMCR) with a neutral rating saying that while the company has a "solid commercial foundation" with Kimmtrak (tebentafusp) for metastatic uveal melanoma, the value of the drug is already priced into the stock. 
  • The firm does not have a price target.
  • Analyst Paul Jeng noted that "we are seeing fewer sources of commercial outperformance for Kimmtrak as US mUM market penetration continues to plateau, contributions from new ex-US launches taper, and real-world duration of therapy is fully appreciated."
  • He added that Kimmtrak could face competitive pressure from a combination treatment of Ideaya Biosciences (IDYA) darovasertib and Pfizer's (PFE) Xalkori (crizotinib). The combo will have regulatory and data catalysts in 2026 "that may serve as negative lateral catalysts on potential for the regimen to erode Kimmtrak’s market share in HLA-A2+ patients."
